segments.title: Washington Treaty subjects in the series
segments.summary: Dr. Clarke maps the Washington Treaty subjects covered by the equitable-treaty series, from capital ships and carriers to cruisers and fortifications.
segments.body: This short outline points viewers toward the series discussions of Washington Treaty capital ships, aircraft carriers, other vessel categories, and limits on fortifications. It also previews a designed cruiser explored through Ultimate Admiral: Dreadnoughts, connecting treaty rules to the practical choices of warship design.

evidence.note: The transcript lists the Washington Treaty capital-ship, carrier, other-ships, fortifications, and cruiser-design installments.

segments.title: London Treaty subjects and fleet categories
segments.summary: The outline previews how the London treaty discussions move across capital ships, carriers, cruisers, destroyers, sloops, and fortifications.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke lays out a category-by-category route through the London naval treaties, including capital ships, carriers, cruisers, destroyers, sloops, and other vessels. The sequence culminates in fortifications and an overall equitable settlement, showing that treaty design must balance several connected parts of fleet structure rather than treating warship types in isolation.

evidence.note: The transcript enumerates the London Treaty installments and the fleet categories each will cover.
